title: 谋杀启事
summary:
During a discussion regarding a woman's appearance, a character notes that while Miss Blacklock described her as short and dark, a photograph depicts a tall, pretty girl with her hair styled up. Inspector Round discusses the lack of information regarding Dmitri Stanfield with Miss Marple, who directs him to a letter concerning Randall Goodale's investigation of Stanfield.

A telephone call from Ridestone to Kollerstrom reveals that Ronald Hammers, a deserting army captain and husband of Phyllis Hyde, has died at Milchester General Hospital following a truck accident in which he heroically saved a child. Ridestone notes that Hammers possessed money and an old ticket to Chipping Craighorne, though the accident occurred before a robbery at a small farm, ruling Hammers out of that incident. Kollerstrom intends to inform Phyllis of the death.

Later, while Miss Marple is knitting, a cat named Tiggy bites through a lamp wire. When Yuan Yuan attempts to use the lamp, water from a vase causes a spark that blows the fuse, plunging the room into darkness. Miss Marple declines a replacement lamp, choosing instead to sit in the dark and write cryptic notes.

At Grasmere Hall, Miss Quincannon and Miss Mulgrew discuss the investigation. Quincannon concludes that Amy Mulgrew is innocent of holding a door for the killer, theorizing instead that the killer used the second door in the living room. They suggest that the person who fired a shot also placed two poisoned aspirins on Miss Blacklock's bedside table, which killed Dora Bunner. They consider whether the culprit acted during the birthday party, noting that while many guests went to the bathroom, the act would be difficult to perform without being seen.
